title: tanacetum densum subsp. amani
intro: Tanacetum densum subsp. amani, also known as Tansy, is a flowering plant belonging to the daisy family, native to Europe, Asia, and North Africa. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to act as an anthelmintic, antiseptic, and anti-inflammatory agent, making it effective in treating a range of health issues. The health benefits of Tansy include its potential to alleviate symptoms of digestive disorders, such as bloating and gas, as well as its ability to reduce fever and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections. Tansy can be used in various herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
